brand.subtitle: AI CLI completion notifications
section.channels.title: Channels
section.sources.title: Sources
section.watch.title: Interactive watch
section.watch.sub: For interactive mode / VSCode extensions: watch local logs and notify after each reply
section.test.title: Test notification
section.summary.title: AI Summary
section.summary.sub: Used to generate a short summary; if it times out, the original task is used.
section.advanced.title: System
section.advanced.sub: Startup, close behavior, and window focus preferences
section.aboutProject.title: About Project
section.aboutProject.sub: Learn about the project, contact the author, and support ongoing maintenance.
aboutProject.projectDesc: AI CLI Complete Notify sends completion reminders for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. It is built for long-running tasks, background watching, and multi-channel notifications.
aboutProject.contactDesc: For feedback, usage questions, or follow-up discussion, you can contact the author on WeChat.
aboutProject.rewardTitle: If this project saved you time
aboutProject.rewardDesc: The project is maintained with bug fixes and compatibility updates. Optional support is appreciated.
aboutProject.alipayDesc: Scan with Alipay to support the project.
aboutProject.wechatPayDesc: Scan with WeChat Pay to support the project.
watch.logRetentionHint: Keep only the last N days of local watch logs
watch.hooksModeHint: Hybrid mode is active: Claude / Gemini / OpenCode prefer hooks/plugins for instant alerts; Codex continues to use watch. Both paths can run together.
watch.hint: Keep watch running in the background. Codex depends on it; for Claude / Gemini it is still a fallback when hooks are missing or do not fire.
summary.apiUrlRuleText: Do not end a base URL with a trailing /, or /v1/chat/completions will not be appended. Add a trailing # only when the full endpoint should be used exactly.
summary.test.emptySummary: No summary returned. Check API URL/key/model.
summary.test.unsupported: Summary test is unavailable in this build.
summary.timeoutHint: Falls back to the default task description if it times out
summary.includeOutputWhenSummaryHint: When enabled, webhooks include the original output after a successful AI summary. When disabled, they show the summary only.
summary.hint: Fill in the API settings to enable summaries. It falls back automatically on timeout.
advanced.useFeishuCard: Use Feishu card format for Webhook
advanced.closeHint: If set to "Minimize to tray", closing the window keeps the app running in the Windows system tray or the macOS menu bar.
advanced.autostart: Launch at login (run in background after login)
advanced.autostartHint: Supported on Windows/macOS; Linux requires manual setup.
advanced.silentStart: Silent start (hide to tray on launch)
advanced.silentStartHint: Takes effect next launch and skips the tray balloon.
close.detail: Minimize to the tray/menu bar to keep running, or quit to stop watchers.
close.remember: Remember my choice (change later in Advanced)
channel.webhook.desc: Generic webhook (Feishu post format; supports multiple URLs)
sources.thresholdHint: Only notify if duration exceeds this value. Click "Save" after changing.
section.sound.title: Sound
section.sound.sub: TTS and custom sound settings (WAV supported)
watch.confirmUsageHint: Turn on to get alerted when Codex needs your input (Plan option prompt/submit, or a turn-ending confirm question). It won't trigger on streaming text. When disabled, confirm prompts won't notify and won't be treated as completion.
advanced.forceMaximize: Force maximize on return (WSL / terminal too)
advanced.forceMaximizeHint: If minimized during the task, clicking the notification restores maximized.
advanced.focusHint: Click the notification to return (more reliable). OS focus rules may still block; for VSCode extensions choose the VSCode target.
advanced.soundTtsHint: Disable to play simple sound only.
advanced.soundCustomHint: WAV supported. You can browse any file. In WSL use Windows path or /mnt/c/.
section.hooks.title: Hooks / Plugin Integration
section.hooks.sub: Claude Code / Gemini CLI use native hooks, OpenCode uses a global plugin, and Codex uses watch. Hybrid mode lets them run together.
hooks.mode.watch.desc: Claude / Gemini / Codex use log polling. Broad compatibility, but Claude defaults to a 60s debounce so alerts can lag. Installed Claude/Gemini hooks are suppressed in this mode.
hooks.mode.hooks.desc: Claude / Gemini / OpenCode prefer hooks/plugins for instant alerts; Codex keeps using watch. Both can run at the same time; same-completion duplicates are suppressed.
hooks.modeHint: Hybrid mode lets Watch and Hooks run together (Codex watch + Claude/Gemini/OpenCode hooks). Watch-only suppresses Claude/Gemini hooks so the two modes stay distinct.
hooks.hint: Windows users can click Install directly. Linux/macOS users can also copy the generated config or plugin file manually. OpenCode writes a global plugin into ~/.config/opencode/plugins/.
